_doc: Rejected topics + reasons, so weak ideas are never re-evaluated. When the scoring system (scoring_sheet.md) kills a topic, record it here. rejection_reason is one of: credibility_issue, weak_hook, duplicate_topic, poor_visuals, low_search_interest.

rejected.notes: The 1930 'whole Inuit village vanished' story traces to a single sensational newspaper writer (Emmett Kelleher) and was debunked by the RCMP. Fails the §6 credibility floor — only ship if framed explicitly as a debunked legend, not a real disappearance.
No verifiable artifacts or sources; widely considered a hoax. Cannot clear the credibility floor.
'Out-of-place' hammer is explained by ordinary concretion; promoted mainly by young-earth sources. Off-brand and uncredible.
No contemporary documentation; classic urban legend. Only usable as an explicitly-labeled 'famous legend' piece, not as a real event.
No single named mystery, no verifiable claim, no 'sounds fake but true' payoff. Vague.
